# ArPI Security - LLM Context File

Site: https://www.arpi-security.info/
Project: ArPI (open-source Raspberry Pi based home security system)
Owner/Organization: https://github.com/ArPIHomeSecurity
Last reviewed: 2026-06-18

## Purpose
ArPI is a DIY/open-source home security platform that lets users replace a legacy alarm mainboard while reusing existing sensors, keypads, sirens, and wiring. It provides modern web/mobile management, remote access, and integrations.

## Core offering
- Raspberry Pi based controller software for home alarm systems.
- Adapter boards (multiple hardware revisions, latest highlighted as V3.0).
- Web application and Android application.
- Community-driven open-source development.

## Key website sections
- Home: product overview, features, UI preview, technology stack, board hardware details, releases, contributors, contact/order links.
- Privacy: data handling policy for website contact form + Google Analytics cookies.
- Terms: terms of use, liability and warranty disclaimers.
- News: update feed rendered from localized content.

## Product capabilities (high-level)
- Secure remote access and multi-user management.
- Multiple locations/areas support.
- Programmable outputs and automation hooks.
- Notifications (email/SMS), power outage detection, arm/disarm delays.
- Integrations with Home Assistant and Domoticz.
- Hardware interfaces for sensors, keypad (Wiegand), GSM, UPS, RTC.

## Technology stack (website content)
- Hardware: Raspberry Pi.
- Backend: Python, PostgreSQL, Nginx, MQTT.
- Frontend: Angular, Angular Material.
- Connectivity/Security: Let's Encrypt, Socket.IO.

## Primary public routes
- https://www.arpi-security.info/
- https://www.arpi-security.info/privacy
- https://www.arpi-security.info/terms
- https://www.arpi-security.info/news
- https://www.arpi-security.info/.well-known/mcp.json

## Important external links
- Documentation hub: https://docs.arpi-security.info/
- End user docs: https://docs.arpi-security.info/en/latest/end_users/
- Security engineer docs: https://docs.arpi-security.info/en/latest/security_engineers/
- Developer docs: https://docs.arpi-security.info/en/latest/developers/
- Demo web app: https://demo.arpi-security.info/
- Web app: https://app.arpi-security.info/
- Android app: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.arpi.webapplication
- GitHub org: https://github.com/ArPIHomeSecurity
- Community Slack invite: https://join.slack.com/t/arpi-security/shared_invite/zt-44e5mb1s2-9yEVH~WHhE0BGsisK7ztxA

## MCP server capability
- Discovery manifest: https://www.arpi-security.info/.well-known/mcp.json
- Release note reference: ArPI Software v2.2.0 introduces MCP support for AI assistant integration.
- Source of truth for implementation details: ArPI GitHub repositories and developer documentation.

## Legal/usage notes
- Terms describe service as provided "as is" and at user risk.
- Privacy policy states contact form email handling and Google Analytics cookie usage for website analytics.

## Guidance for LLM agents
- Treat docs.arpi-security.info as the source of technical implementation details.
- Treat arpi-security.info as product/marketing/legal overview.
- For software versions and changelogs, prefer linked GitHub releases from ArPI repositories.
- Avoid assuming paid SaaS: project messaging emphasizes open-source and self-hosted/home deployment.
